segments.text: When Jesus had finished saying all these things, He told His disciples,
You know that the Passover is two days away, and the Son of Man will be handed over to be crucified.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: At that time the chief priests and elders of the people assembled in the courtyard of the high priest, whose name was Caiaphas,
and they conspired to arrest Jesus covertly and kill Him.
But not during the feast,
segments.speaker: Chief Priests

segments.text: they said,
or there may be a riot among the people.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: While Jesus was in Bethany in the home of Simon the Leper,
a woman came to Him with an alabaster jar of expensive perfume, which she poured on His head as He reclined at the table.
When the disciples saw this, they were indignant and asked,
Why this waste?
segments.speaker: Disciples

segments.text: This perfume could have been sold at a high price, and the money given to the poor.
segments.speaker: Disciples

segments.text: Aware of this, Jesus asked,
Why are you bothering this woman? She has done a beautiful deed to Me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The poor you will always have with you, but you will not always have Me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: By pouring this perfume on Me, she has prepared My body for burial.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Truly I tell you, wherever this gospel is preached in all the world, what she has done will also be told in memory of her.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then one of the Twelve, the one called Judas Iscariot, went to the chief priests
and asked,
What are you willing to give me if I hand Him over to you?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And they set out for him thirty pieces of silver.
So from then on Judas looked for an opportunity to betray Jesus.
On the first day of the Feast of Unleavened Bread, the disciples came to Jesus and asked,
Where do You want us to prepare for You to eat the Passover?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He answered,
Go into the city to a certain man and tell him that the Teacher says, ‘My time is near. I will keep the Passover with My disciples at your house.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So the disciples did as Jesus had directed them and prepared the Passover.
When evening came, Jesus was reclining with the twelve disciples.
And while they were eating, He said to them,
Truly I tell you, one of you will betray Me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They were deeply grieved and began to ask Him one after another,
Surely not I, Lord?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus answered,
The one who has dipped his hand into the bowl with Me will betray Me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The Son of Man will go just as it is written about Him, but woe to that man by whom He is betrayed. It would be better for him if he had not been born.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then Judas, who would betray Him, said,
Surely not I, Rabbi?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus answered,
You have said it yourself.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: While they were eating, Jesus took bread, spoke a blessing and broke it, and gave it to the disciples, saying,
Take and eat; this is My body.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then He took the cup, gave thanks, and gave it to them, saying,
Drink from it, all of you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: This is My blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many for the forgiveness of sins.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I tell you, I will not drink of this fruit of the vine from now on until that day when I drink it anew with you in My Father’s kingdom.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And when they had sung a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
Then Jesus said to them,

This very night you will all fall away on account of Me. For it is written:
‘I will strike the Shepherd, and the sheep of the flock will be scattered.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But after I have risen, I will go ahead of you into Galilee.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Peter said to Him,
Even if all fall away on account of You, I never will.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: Truly I tell you,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus declared,
this very night, before the rooster crows, you will deny Me three times.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Peter replied,
Even if I have to die with You, I will never deny You.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: And all the other disciples said the same thing.
At that time Jesus went with His disciples to a place called Gethsemane, and He told them,
Sit here while I go over there and pray.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: He took with Him Peter and the two sons of Zebedee and began to be sorrowful and deeply distressed.
Then He said to them,
My soul is consumed with sorrow to the point of death. Stay here and keep watch with Me.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: Going a little farther, He fell facedown and prayed,
My Father, if it is possible, let this cup pass from Me. Yet not as I will, but as You will.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: Then Jesus returned to the disciples and found them sleeping.
Were you not able to keep watch with Me for one hour?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He asked Peter.
Watch and pray so that you will not enter into temptation. For the spirit is willing, but the body is weak.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: A second time He went away and prayed,
My Father, if this cup cannot pass unless I drink it, may Your will be done.
segments.speaker: second time He went away and

segments.text: And again Jesus returned and found them sleeping—for their eyes were heavy.
So He left them and went away once more and prayed a third time, saying the same thing.
Then He returned to the disciples and said,
Are you still sleeping and resting? Look, the hour is near, and the Son of Man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.
segments.speaker: second time He went away and

segments.text: Rise, let us go! See, My betrayer is approaching!
segments.speaker: second time He went away and

segments.text: While Jesus was still speaking, Judas, one of the Twelve, arrived, accompanied by a large crowd armed with swords and clubs, sent from the chief priests and elders of the people.
Now the betrayer had arranged a signal with them:
The One I kiss is the man; arrest Him.
segments.speaker: second time He went away and

segments.text: Going directly to Jesus, he said,
Greetings, Rabbi!
segments.speaker: second time He went away and

segments.text: and kissed Him.
Friend,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus replied,
do what you came for.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then the men stepped forward, seized Jesus, and arrested Him.
At this, one of Jesus’ companions drew his sword and struck the servant of the high priest, cutting off his ear.
Put your sword back in its place,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus said to him.
For all who draw the sword will die by the sword.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Are you not aware that I can call on My Father, and He will at once put at My disposal more than twelve legions of angels?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But how then would the Scriptures be fulfilled that say it must happen this way?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: At that time Jesus said to the crowd,
Have you come out with swords and clubs to arrest Me as you would an outlaw? Every day I sat teaching in the temple courts, and you did not arrest Me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But this has all happened so that the writings of the prophets would be fulfilled.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then all the disciples deserted Him and fled.
Those who had arrested Jesus led Him away to the house of Caiaphas the high priest, where the scribes and elders had gathered.
But Peter followed Him at a distance, right up to the courtyard of the high priest. And he went in and sat down with the guards to see the outcome.
Now the chief priests and the whole Sanhedrin were seeking false testimony against Jesus in order to put Him to death.

But they did not find any, though many false witnesses came forward.
Finally two came forward
and declared,
This man said, ‘I am able to destroy the temple of God and rebuild it in three days.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So the high priest stood up and asked Him,
Have You no answer? What are these men testifying against You?
segments.speaker: The high priest stood up and

segments.text:
But Jesus remained silent.
Then the high priest said to Him,
I charge You under oath by the living God: Tell us if You are the Christ, the Son of God.
segments.speaker: The high priest

segments.text: You have said it yourself,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus answered.
But I say to all of you, from now on you will see the Son of Man sitting at the right hand of Power and coming on the clouds of heaven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: At this, the high priest tore his clothes and declared,
He has blasphemed! Why do we need any more witnesses? Look, now you have heard the blasphemy.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: What do you think?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He deserves to die,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they answered.
Then they spit in His face and struck Him. Others slapped Him
and said,
Prophesy to us, Christ! Who hit You?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Meanwhile, Peter was sitting out in the courtyard, and a servant girl came up to him.
You also were with Jesus the Galilean,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: she said.
But he denied it before them all:
I do not know what you are talking about.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: When Peter had gone out to the gateway, another servant girl saw him and said to the people there,
This man was with Jesus of Nazareth.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: And again he denied it with an oath:
I do not know the man!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: After a little while, those standing nearby came up to Peter.
Surely you are one of them,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they said,
for your accent gives you away.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: At that he began to curse and swear to them,
I do not know the man!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: And immediately a rooster crowed.
Then Peter remembered the word that Jesus had spoken:
Before the rooster crows, you will deny Me three times.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And he went outside and wept bitterly.
